$meta.design_language: Apple Liquid Glass — iOS 27 generation (WWDC 2026), adapted for web
$meta.source: Claude-Design-UIUX-Brief.md §2 (palette lifted from AI-SAP-Pricing-Framework.pptx); do not invent colors

navy.role: Primary brand surface — chrome backdrop, hero panels, dark sections. 60–70% of visual weight.

ice.role: Light panel fill, text on navy, card borders

gold.role: THE accent — primary actions, active states, key numbers, focus rings. Keep < ~10% of a screen.

red.role: Semantic: blocked / hard failure / destructive ONLY

green.role: Semantic: pass / within policy ONLY (+ the one sanctioned non-semantic use: on-device privacy chip)

tint_semantics_glass.note: Fixed list — a fifth tint requires the architect's sign-off

typography.family_serif_proposal: Noto Serif / Noto Serif JP (customer paper document only)
rules: Uppercase tracked labels (letter-spacing:.14em) for section kickers only — never body
JA kicker: same gold-deep + size, NORMAL tracking, no uppercase; optional leading dot ・
JA body line-height 1.7 (vs 1.5 Latin); no italics in JA (use weight/color)
Numerals stay Latin + tabular in both locales

glass.note: Navigation-layer material ONLY. Dark navy chrome. Three user-controlled intensity stops (iOS 27 slider). All glass flows from these tokens so a future tuning pass edits tokens, not components.

opaque.note: reuses the reduced-transparency fallback — OS setting and app 'Opaque' stop converge on one look

dimming_tiers.over-busy: step up dimming over charts/dense tables so text-on-glass keeps WCAG AA

i18n.currency: follows the DEAL, not the UI language (USD $1,234,567 ; JPY ¥1,234,567 no decimals)
i18n.formatting: always via Intl.* APIs, never hand-drawn; timestamps store UTC, display in user tz
i18n.proposal: renders in the CUSTOMER's locale independent of author UI language

themes.note: Content-surface tokens per theme (chrome glass is theme-independent — see glass{}). Each screen sets these as CSS custom properties on the page root via the pageStyle hole; content styles use var(--token, <light fallback>). Architect-approved (2026-07): dark stays navy-anchored, no new hues, gold + red/green semantics unchanged, re-prove AA both glass extremes in the annex.

chart_navy_on_dark.note: navy-2 (#24385C) is invisible on dark content surfaces — data-viz navy uses these, readable on both themes

responsive.mobile: glass bottom tab bar (.ds-tabbar) replaces the rail; ≤5 simultaneous glass surfaces (architect cap — consolidate IA, never raise); tables scroll via .ds-tablewrap/.ds-mxwrap; Evidence capture rises as a bottom sheet (morph #4)

morphs: #2 capsule → packaging → streaming → solid Markdown card (Insights)

risk_adjusted_max.binding: PLACEHOLDER ONLY. Engine-bound — computed per deal by compute(); never a fixed policy constant. Per architect ruling 2026-07, screens showing it (Deal Workspace governance banner, Approvals metric grid) must annotate 'engine-bound · per-deal' in redlines.
